mechanism_narrative: A persistent multi-month rise in FEDFUNDS reflects an active tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during rising-rate months are not significantly lower than during flat/falling-rate months (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection in labor market slack that triggers rapid risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the effect disappears once controlling for FEDFUNDS trend (partial correlation p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in INDPRO growth together with rising CPIAUCSL signals an overheating supply-side environment where input-cost pressure erodes margins faster than output growth compensates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Neither series alone captures this margin-compression channel.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 3%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show 60d forward returns not statistically different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series INDPRO or CPIAUCSL models alone achieve equal or higher Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months), a simultaneous decline in HOUST signals that higher rates are transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, foreshadowing broader demand deceleration and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Outside tightening cycles, HOUST declines do not carry the same forward signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months AND HOUST falls > 5% over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: During tightening-cycle regimes, 20d forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are not significantly more negative than non-triggered HOUST declines outside tightening (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in PAYEMS month-over-month growth reflects expanding labor income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS trend versus 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in more than one of three consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS exceeds a restrictive threshold of 4.5%, further increases sharply raise real borrowing costs, triggering a nonlinear repricing of long-duration equity cash flows that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Below this threshold, rate changes have muted equity impact.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns above the 4.5% threshold are not significantly more negative than returns below the threshold (p > 0.05), or the relationship is linear rather than exhibiting a threshold break in piecewise regression.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth accelerates while CPIAUCSL inflation remains subdued, it signals real consumption strength without offsetting price erosion, driving a short-term positive reaction in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days. If inflation is elevated alongside strong sales, the same RSAFS strength does not translate into positive returns because real purchasing power gains are illusory.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation < 0.2%
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following joint triggers are not significantly higher than returns following RSAFS strength with elevated inflation (p > 0.05), or single-series RSAFS momentum alone matches joint-trigger Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints and an impending industrial slowdown, leading markets to discount future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). In early-cycle regimes, the same deceleration reflects normalization rather than distress and carries no negative signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth decelerates by more than 2pp
failure_condition: 60d forward returns following INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle regimes are not significantly more negative than in early-cycle regimes (p > 0.05), or the effect is indistinguishable from the unconditional INDPRO trend relationship.
